name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
note: First hybrid-reasoning Claude with visible extended thinking; launched alongside Claude Code; retired Feb 2026.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, vanilla agent (no extra scaffold); confirmed on official announcement

GPQA Diamond, standard mode (no thinking); original deeplearning.ai URL only showed the 84.8% extended-thinking figure in extracted text, so sourceUrl swapped to DataCamp which explicitly states 68.0% standard-mode
AIME 2024, standard mode (no thinking); same sourceUrl correction as GPQA row, confirmed 23.3% standard-mode on DataCamp

Multilingual Q&A (MMMLU), standard mode / no extended thinking, from Anthropic's official benchmark-comparison chart on the announcement page. Extended-thinking mode scores 86.1% (not used, to stay consistent with the standard-mode convention of the existing 3 rows).
Visual reasoning, MMMU (validation), standard mode / no extended thinking. Extended-thinking mode scores 75%.
Instruction-following (IFEval), standard mode / no extended thinking. Extended-thinking mode scores 93.2%.
MATH 500 (500 competition math problems), standard mode / no extended thinking. Extended-thinking mode scores 96.2%.

TAU-bench retail domain, standard mode / no extended thinking; filed under this slug per existing corpus convention (airline-domain 58.4% from the same chart omitted to avoid a duplicate-key collision, matching how claude-3-5-sonnet-new.json handles the same benchmark).

verificationNotes: Real model, confirmed via anthropic.com/news/claude-3-7-sonnet (release 2025-02-24, 200K context, $3/$15 pricing, launched alongside Claude Code). Per Anthropic's official model-deprecations page (fetched 2026-07-12), claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219 was deprecated 2025-10-28 and RETIRED 2026-02-19 — since today is 2026-07-12 this model is now fully retired, not merely deprecated; apiAvailable corrected to false (schema has no 'retired' status enum, so status kept as 'deprecated', the closest option). SWE-bench 63.7% confirmed on the primary announcement. GPQA/AIME standard-mode figures (68%/23.3%) were NOT literally present in the given deeplearning.ai sourceUrl's extracted text (it only showed extended-thinking scores); confirmed the exact same numbers on DataCamp's writeup instead and swapped sourceUrl accordingly rather than dropping real, verifiable data.
